source: Yang Zhai San Yao (Three Essentials of Yang Dwellings), Classical Practice

rules.description: Main door should be the largest door in the building
rules.notes: The main entrance must be proportionally dominant. Interior doors should not exceed front door size.

rules.description: Main door height should be proportional to building — Lu Ban ruler auspicious dimensions
rules.notes: Lu Ban ruler divides measurements into 8 sectors cycling through auspicious/inauspicious. Common auspicious door heights: 2110mm, 2134mm, 2286mm.

rules.description: Lu Ban ruler auspicious width ranges (first cycle)
rules.notes: The 8 sectors per 432mm cycle: Cai (wealth 0-54), Bing (illness 54-108), Li (separation 108-162), Yi (righteousness 162-215), Guan (authority 215-269), Jie (robbery 269-323), Hai (harm 323-378), Ben (origin 378-432). Cycle repeats.

rules.description: Front door must not directly align with back door (qi piercing)
rules.notes: Direct front-to-back alignment causes qi to rush through without settling. Cure: place a screen, partition, or stagger the alignment.

rules.description: Front door must not directly face a staircase
rules.notes: Stairs rushing down toward the door drain qi out. Stairs should be offset or screened from direct door view.

rules.description: Front door must not directly face a toilet door
rules.notes: Toilet represents draining/flushing qi. Keep toilet doors closed and not visible from main entry.

rules.description: Front door must not directly face a mirror
rules.notes: Mirror reflects qi back out the door. Place mirrors on side walls, never facing the entrance.

rules.description: Avoid three or more doors in a straight line (qi arrow)
rules.notes: Multiple aligned doors accelerate qi into sha. Stagger door placements or use screens to break the line.

rules.description: Entry foyer should be bright and well-lit
rules.notes: Dark entryways suppress incoming qi. Use lighting, light colors, and if possible natural light.

rules.description: Entry area should be clutter-free with clear circulation
rules.notes: Clutter at the qi mouth blocks energy distribution. Maintain minimum 1.2m clear width at entry.

rules.description: Entry should not open directly into a wall less than 1.5m away
rules.notes: Facing a wall immediately upon entering blocks qi and creates oppressive feeling. If unavoidable, hang artwork or use a plant.

rules.description: Door facing should ideally match the owner's favorable direction (sheng qi, tian yi, yan nian, or fu wei)
rules.notes: Calculate owner's Kua number from birth year. East group (Kua 1,3,4,9): favor E,SE,N,S. West group (Kua 2,6,7,8): favor W,NW,SW,NE.

rules.description: Kua number calculation for males born before 2000
Kua number calculation for females born before 2000
Two doors facing each other across a corridor cause quarrels
rules.notes: Offset doors slightly or hang curtains. If doors are different sizes, the larger one dominates.

rules.description: Door should not be directly under an exposed beam
rules.notes: Beam above the door creates pressing qi. Cover the beam with a false ceiling or hang flutes.
Inward-opening door draws qi into the building. Outward-opening pushes qi away. Commercial may differ due to fire code requirements — fire code always takes precedence.
